title: What Is Your Job Now, Farhan Thawar | Compile 26
summary: Farhan Thawar, head of engineering at Shopify, discusses how engineers' roles change when AI writes most code. The conversation covers shifting software development lifecycle practices, learning as a collateral cost, moving bottlenecks in engineering, and the differences between prototyping and production in an AI-assisted workflow.
writeup: Farhan Thawar, head of engineering at Shopify, examines how the engineering role changes once AI writes most code, in a Compile 26 talk hosted by Cursor. The discussion covers shifting software development lifecycle practices, learning treated as a collateral cost, and how bottlenecks move across the engineering process rather than disappearing. It also draws a line between prototyping and production in an AI-assisted workflow, touching on test-time compute and human-in-the-loop oversight.
